meta.name: Garlic Mashed Potatoes
meta.subtitle: Creamy mash with mellow garlic, butter, and fresh herbs (Herb & Garlic style)

source.name: ssa.cuisines.herb_garlic
notes: Canonical SSA Herb & Garlic baseline mashed potatoes.
Built for deterministic planning + adaptation (dairy-free option included).

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a side (about 3/4 cup each).

required.label: Potato masher or ricer
required.notes: A ricer gives the smoothest mash. A masher is classic and still great.

optional.label: Small saucepan
optional.notes: Nice for warming milk/cream and melting butter together.

optional.label: Hand mixer (optional)
optional.notes: Use carefully on low. Overmixing can make potatoes gluey.

ingredients.label: Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and cut into 1.5-inch chunks
notes: Yukon Golds give a naturally buttery texture.
Russets also work: expect fluffier, slightly drier mash.

ingredients.label: Garlic cloves
notes: Boiling garlic with potatoes mellows the bite.
For stronger garlic: add 1–2 cloves grated into the finished mash (optional).

ingredients.label: Kosher salt (for water + seasoning)
notes: Salt the water generously for best flavor. Adjust final salt to taste.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter
notes: Add more for richer mash if desired.

ingredients.label: Whole milk (or half-and-half/cream)
notes: Warm before adding for the smoothest mash.
Start with 1/2 cup and add more to reach your preferred texture.

ingredients.label: Sour cream (optional)
notes: Adds tang and extra creaminess. Greek yogurt also works.

ingredients.label: Chives, finely sliced
notes: Optional but excellent for garlic mash.

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil (optional finish)
notes: Optional Herb & Garlic flourish: drizzle on top with herbs.

steps.title: Boil potatoes and garlic
instructions: Add potatoes and whole garlic cloves to a large pot. Cover with cold water by about 1 inch.
Add about 1 tsp of the salt to the water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer until potatoes are fork-tender (about 15–18 minutes).
checks: Potatoes are fork-tender (knife slides in easily)

steps.title: Warm milk and melt butter
instructions: While potatoes cook, warm the milk (or cream) until just hot (not boiling).
Melt butter into the warm milk if you like (optional but helps incorporation).

steps.title: Drain and dry the potatoes
instructions: Drain potatoes and garlic well in a colander.
Return them to the hot pot and set over low heat for 1–2 minutes, shaking occasionally, to evaporate excess water.
checks: Steam rises and excess moisture evaporates

steps.title: Mash, then enrich
instructions: Mash potatoes and garlic until mostly smooth (or pass through a ricer for very smooth mash).
Add butter and warm milk gradually, stirring gently until creamy.
Optional: fold in sour cream for extra tang/creaminess.
Season with remaining salt (to taste) and black pepper.

steps.title: Finish with herbs (optional) and serve
instructions: Stir in parsley and/or chives if using.
Optional: drizzle a little olive oil on top and add a pinch of herbs for a Herb & Garlic finish.

refrigerator.guidance: Cool quickly and refrigerate in an airtight container. Reheat gently with a splash of milk.

freezer.guidance: Freezing is possible but texture may change. Freeze in portions; reheat with extra milk and butter.

best: Stovetop on low with a splash of milk, stirring often
Microwave in short bursts, stirring, with milk added
avoid: High heat without added liquid (can scorch or dry out)

batchNotes: Great for meal prep: make ahead and reheat with milk/butter.
Hold warm (covered) up to 60 minutes; stir occasionally and add milk if thickening.
leftovers: Make potato cakes (pan-sear with a little oil/butter)

summary: Creamy garlic mashed potatoes with mellow boiled garlic, butter, and optional fresh herbs—an Herb & Garlic side that pairs with roasts, fish, and stews.

handling: Cool leftovers within 2 hours and refrigerate promptly.
Reheat until steaming hot; add milk as needed to restore texture.

notes: Roast 1 head of garlic at 400°F for ~35–45 minutes until soft.
Squeeze cloves into the mash and reduce boiled garlic (or omit).
Replace butter with 3 tbsp olive oil.
Replace milk with warm chicken/vegetable broth (start with 1/2 cup and add as needed).
Finish with herbs and extra olive oil drizzle.
Add 1 tbsp chopped parsley + 1 tbsp chives + 1 tsp thyme leaves.
Finish with lemon zest (small pinch) for a brighter Herb & Garlic profile.
